summary: Decompiled a large portion of melee/lb/lbsnap.c, replacing many stubbed lbSnap functions with typed implementations for snapshot memory-card state, file naming/deletion/copying, snapshot metadata, icon conversion, archive initialization, and save/load plumbing. The PR also refined lbsnap and lbcardgame prototypes, added supporting baselib prototypes/macros, renamed the lbsnap BSS object to lbSnap_80433380, and corrected the lbsnap sdata split/symbol size for lbSnap_804D3828. The decomp.dev report showed +624 matched bytes overall and several lbsnap functions reaching 100%.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed lbsnap functions including lbSnap_8001D394, lbSnap_8001D3B0, lbSnap_8001D3CC, lbSnap_8001D3E8, lbSnap_8001D40C, lbSnap_8001D4A4, lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001D7B0, lbSnap_8001DA5C, lbSnap_8001DC0C, lbSnap_8001DE8C, lbSnap_8001DF20, lbSnap_8001DF6C, lbSnap_8001E058, lbSnap_8001E218, and lbSnap_8001E290.
Renamed the static BSS object from lb_80433380 to lbSnap_80433380 in symbols and code, making ownership of the global snapshot state clearer.

Recovered lbsnap static structures: Unk80433380_48_10, expanded Unk80433380_48 from padding to fields plus a 0x7F-entry array, added Unk80433380_0, and modeled Unk803BACC8 with known fields used by snapshot save operations.
Changed lbsnap.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete return and argument types for most lbSnap functions.
Refined lbcardgame.h prototypes for the card/file APIs used by lbsnap, including lb_8001B99C, lb_8001BB48, lb_8001BF04, lb_8001BFD8, lb_8001C0F4, and lb_8001C4A8.
Added HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, cond, msg) in baselib/debug.h so decompiled assertions can use a literal message string instead of stringifying the condition.
Added hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B5C2C, and hsd_803B6BE4 declarations in baselib/hsd_3B34.h for snapshot capture/processing calls.
Corrected config/GALE01/splits.txt lbsnap .sdata end from 0x804D3830 to 0x804D382B and changed lbSnap_804D3828 in symbols.txt from an 0x8 object to a 0x3 string.
Implemented snapshot icon pixel conversion logic from RGB565 to RGB5A3 using explicit masks and a RGB565_TO_RGB5A3 macro.
Added snapshot timestamp/name formatting using OSGetTime, OSTicksToSeconds, OSSecondsToTicks, OSTicksToCalendarTime, sprintf, and language-dependent Japanese/English text.
smart_moves: Recovered structure layout before broad function decompilation, which allowed many accessors and card-state functions to become simple field reads/writes instead of opaque pointer arithmetic.
Kept static asserts on recovered structs, especially Unk80433380_48_10, Unk80433380_48, and Unk80433380, to preserve binary layout confidence while replacing padding.
Used a domain-specific symbol rename, lbSnap_80433380, rather than retaining a generic lb_ address name, improving future searchability and reducing confusion with neighboring lb globals.
Introduced HSD_ASSERTMSG instead of forcing assertions through HSD_ASSERT when the original binary appears to use a fixed message string.
Documented the RGB565 to RGB5A3 conversion with comments and masks, making a low-level matching tactic understandable and reusable.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with PAD_STACK(8) in functions that build local text buffers.
Left evidence-grounded names where semantics were still unclear, such as unk8, unkC, and unk10, instead of over-naming unknown fields.
decomp_lessons: For library modules around memory-card data, refine dependent header prototypes first; accurate signatures for lbcardgame calls unlocked cleaner lbsnap implementations.
Snapshot/card functions often use state-change gates: if per-channel card state changed while no error is active, the code sets the per-slot status to 8 before proceeding.

When reconstructing save-file names, the code uses zeroed 0x21 buffers and decimal timestamps from OSTicksToSeconds/OSGetTime; duplicate timestamps are avoided by incrementing until no existing entry matches.
Index-bound assertions may need a macro that accepts a custom message string; stringified conditions are not always sufficient for matching original HSD assertion calls.
Texture/icon conversion code can be clarified through bit-mask macros without hiding the original arithmetic, helping preserve both matchability and readability.
Config metadata can change during decompilation: symbol ownership/name and section sizes should be updated when a global is identified as a smaller string or belongs to a specific module.
Use concrete array lengths from evidence, such as 0x7F snapshot entries and 2 card channels, and protect recovered sizes with STATIC_ASSERT.
naming_conventions: Module-owned static globals use the module prefix and address, e.g. lbSnap_80433380 and lbSnap_803BACC8.
Recovered struct fields use offset-prefixed names when semantics are partial, e.g. x0, x4_string, x44_LbMcSnap_MemSnapIconData, x4C_cardState, and x54_stateChanged.
Unknown nested fields retain conservative names such as unk0, unk8, unkC, and unk10 until their meanings are proven.
Function prototypes use concrete C types where known, replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS in public headers.
Arguments are renamed when the role is clear enough, e.g. chan for memory-card channel and index/jndex for snapshot entry indices.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) near local 0x21-byte text buffers to influence stack layout for matching.
Used explicit while loops to zero character buffers instead of library calls, matching the likely generated control flow.
Represented RGB565-to-RGB5A3 conversion with masks and a macro: keep blue bits, shift red/green down by one, and force the RGB5A3 alpha bit opaque.
Kept an int loop variable for channel initialization with a comment that EXIChannel did not optimize the loop properly.
Used pointer-difference arithmetic when preparing lbSnap_803BACC8.x14 from lbSnap_80433380.x0->xC and the offset between x38 and x0.
Used HSD_ASSERTMSG with fixed line 410 and message "index < _p(slot)[chan].num" in several indexed snapshot operations.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased by 624 bytes and overall GALE01 matched code rose by 0.02% to 26.63%.
The bot reported several functions reaching 100%: lbSnap_8001D394, lbSnap_8001D3B0, lbSnap_8001D3CC, lbSnap_8001D3E8, lbSnap_8001D40C, lbSnap_8001DE8C, lbSnap_8001E218, and lbSnap_8001E290.

The bot reported partial progress for lbSnap_8001D4A4, lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001D7B0, lbSnap_8001DC0C, lbSnap_8001DF20, lbSnap_8001DF6C, and lbSnap_8001E058, so not all newly written functions were fully matching according to the report.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching lbSnap_8001D4A4, lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001D7B0, lbSnap_8001DC0C, lbSnap_8001DF20, lbSnap_8001DF6C, or lbSnap_8001E058.
Search for later renames of Unk80433380_48 fields such as unk8, unkC, and unk10 after more memory-card snapshot semantics were recovered.
Check whether hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B5C2C, and hsd_803B6BE4 receive clearer names or types in later baselib decompilation.
Search for other PRs using HSD_ASSERTMSG to confirm whether this macro became the standard pattern for fixed-message assertions.
Compare future snapshot/icon code against the RGB565_TO_RGB5A3 macro and tiling arithmetic introduced here.
